own_review_points: CracRecorder.enabled/fullWarmup are public static non-volatile, written in static-init and read later in Quarkus.manualInitialize() with no visibility guarantee
CracRecorder.register() contains only a commented-out registration block — dead code; registration actually moved to Quarkus.manualInitialize()
Quarkus.manualInitialize() sets manualState=MANUAL_INITIALIZED twice (once after newInstance, again after CRAC register) — redundant
PreloadClassesRecorder.preloadClass uses catch(Throwable ignored), swallowing OOM/VMError and hiding load failures
CracConfig.enable is Optional<Boolean> (tri-state anti-pattern) to express extension-dependent default
CRAC checkpoint/restore with sockets-holding resources will break (no resource cleanup in beforeCheckpoint)
CracProcessor: defaultVal==null can never be true since Quarkus injects Optional via ofNullable
notes: Solid, grounded review. Correctly flags the double manualState assignment (verifiable in diff), Throwable swallowing, dead null check, and non-volatile recorder statics. Several supporting citations (ExtensionLoader, ExecutorRecorder) are outside the diff but the core claims rest on diff code. Point about CracEnabledBuildItem being unconsumed asserts absence outside the diff window (trivial). lineSeparator nit is valid. Missed all 4 still-open human points: Optional<Boolean> anti-pattern, sockets-break concern, the #29016 alternative-design question, and the commented-out/dead registration in register(). net=2.
